summary: TOOL-CONTEXT Phase 0: namespace vs toolId key mismatch fragility
detail: In registry.ts, modeDescriptions is keyed by namespace (tool filename, e.g. "coordinate") at line 415: `modeDescriptions.set(namespace, mod.modeDescriptions)`. But describeTool looks up by toolId at line 714: `s.modeDescriptions.get(toolId)`. For default exports, toolId === namespace (coincidence). For named exports (toolId = `namespace_id`), the lookup would silently return undefined, causing logWarning and empty description. Currently safe because all .opencode/tools/*.ts use default exports. Recommend: use namespace in describeTool lookup or store under toolId.

summary: TOOL-CONTEXT Phase 0: test added to file with pre-existing type errors
detail: registry.test.ts has 3 pre-existing type errors: lines 72 (Layer.mergeAll arg count 21 exceeds 20 limit), 74 (pipe on never), 81 (brokenPluginLayer missing Interface properties). The modeDescriptions test was added at line 564 to this file, meaning the new test cannot be typechecked or executed independently. The test is structurally correct but unverifiable. This is NOT caused by Phase 0 changes.

summary: TOOL-CONTEXT Phase 0: modeDescriptions guard accepts arrays
detail: The guard at registry.ts:414 (`typeof mod.modeDescriptions === "object"`) passes for arrays (`typeof [] === "object"`). If a tool accidentally exports modeDescriptions as an array, it would be silently stored as Record<string,string>, causing `variants[agent.name]` to return undefined at runtime. Recommend: add `!Array.isArray(mod.modeDescriptions)` check.

summary: TOOL-CONTEXT Phase 0: implementation claim inflation (5 vs 3 tool files)
detail: User claims "MODIFIED: 5 .opencode/tools/*.ts" for modeDescriptions. Git diff shows only 3 files actually modified for modeDescriptions: coordinate.ts (+5), read_source.ts (+6), smart_delegate.ts (+5). plan.ts and fragment.ts had modeDescriptions pre-existing at HEAD. roadmap.ts was modified for an unrelated deprecated-alias change.

summary: TOOL-CONTEXT Phase 0: 3 untested code paths in describeTool, zero production-file integration tests
detail: Blind-spot analysis reveals 3 untested code paths in describeTool: (1) warn/fallback when agent name not in modeDescriptions map (registry.ts:720-724) — never exercised, (2) early-return on no variants for a tool ID (L715) — never explicitly verified, (3) TaskTool/SkillTool delegation branches (L711-712) — never asserted against modeDescriptions. Additionally, zero integration tests verify any of the 5 real .opencode/tools/*.ts files (plan, coordinate, smart_delegate, read_source, fragment) actually load and serve correct per-agent descriptions.
